claim: Running 20 OpenClaw agents 24/7 on a Hostinger VPS costs less than $7/month using optimization techniques (e.g., cheaper API routing via OpenRouter)
actionable: Use VPS hosting for multi-agent systems; route API calls through cost-optimized providers; measure daily costs per agent
source_title: I Run 20 OpenClaw Agents 24/7! Here's How to Set up Agent Teams

claim: Orchestrator pattern: one master coordinator routes tasks to specialized agents and collects outputs, enabling team coordination without agents being aware of each other
actionable: Design multi-agent systems with a single orchestrator that routes to specialists; keep agents focused on their domain
source_title: I Run 20 OpenClaw Agents 24/7! Here's How to Set up Agent Teams

claim: Soul.md file defines agent identity and personality; agents.md file specifies tools, triggers, and output locations—separation enables consistent behavior
actionable: Store agent configuration in soul.md (role, personality, rules) and agents.md (tools, triggers); version control these separately from code
source_title: I Run 20 OpenClaw Agents 24/7! Here's How to Set up Agent Teams

claim: Command center dashboard syncs agent outputs every 2 hours, showing pipeline (backlog/in-progress/review/done), approvals, gallery, and costs in one interface
actionable: Build a unified dashboard for multi-agent oversight; sync outputs periodically; expose approval gates and cost tracking
source_title: I Run 20 OpenClaw Agents 24/7! Here's How to Set up Agent Teams

claim: Most creators show surface-level agent examples (news summaries); real utility comes from recurring, time-consuming, rule-based, non-urgent tasks that agents handle in background
actionable: Design agents for background task execution, not chat; target recurring pain points, not showpiece demos
source_title: I Run 20 OpenClaw Agents 24/7! Here's How to Set up Agent Teams
